summary: The article argues that the rush to add generative AI to data products is premature hype that distracts from fundamental unsolved problems like data quality, governance, and basic BI accuracy. The authors contend that most companies can't even get their dashboards to match, yet are being sold AI features that promise 'anyone can talk to their data' - a dangerous proposition when the underlying data foundations are broken.
key_points: 2024 is the year companies are testing GenAI in production and discovering real-world limitations
Most companies still struggle with basic BI accuracy and data quality issues
AI demos use oversimplified questions and clean datasets unrepresentative of real business complexity
Overreliance on AI tools exacerbates the skills gap and makes the industry collectively less technical
The 'chat with your data' feature often lacks a clear user story beyond 'we need AI in our product'
money_quotes: 2024 is the year of f*cking around and finding out how well GenAI works in production.
From what we can tell, most companies are still barely getting BI right. Reports don't match, numbers don't make sense, and the data teams still spend much time cobbling together data.
May as well hand a loaded gun to business stakeholders by passing 'human in the loop' responsibility over to people who are woefully unprepared to suss out when something is blatantly wrong.
It's a wave of 'innovation' that isn't genuinely innovative but leads us down a path of SaaS enshittification.
The user story for most of these features was probably 'As a SaaS vendor, I want to add AI to my product.'
key_insight: Before chasing AI features, companies should focus on solving their fundamental data problems - you can't magically chat with data that's still broken at its foundation.
